title: senecio papposus subsp. congestus
intro: Senecio papposus subsp. congestus, also known as the woolly groundsel, is a subspecies of the Senecio papposus plant, characterized by its unique, woolly leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of woolly groundsel include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe irritated throats. Woolly groundsel can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in herbal remedies, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
